primary_goal: Build authority for UK ecommerce and wholesale automation through high-quality, specific, operator-led articles.
ai_mention_rule: AI may be covered only when it supports controlled workflows, clear review rules and ROI proof.

articles.title: How to reconcile Shopify payouts, orders and fees
articles.topic_type: Commercial operator guide to ecommerce payout reconciliation
articles.trend_search_signal: Official Shopify and Xero sources checked on 20 July 2026. Shopify confirms that the payout reconciliation report explains Shopify Payments balance activity, fees and payouts but is not a revenue statement and excludes third-party processors. Xero confirms that its Shopify integration supports daily summaries or individual sales and payout matching.
articles.primary_search_intent: A UK ecommerce operator or finance lead wants to match Shopify orders, refunds, fees and deposits without rebuilding the month by hand or posting payouts as revenue.
articles.authority_value: Adds a finance-control guide with named source files, payout-ID grouping, parsing and lookup mechanisms, duplicate prevention, two-stage reconciliation, exception ownership, pilot rules and payback maths.
articles.duplicate_check: Passed. Existing finance content covers wholesale order entry into Sage or Xero. This article covers payment settlement, clearing accounts and bank reconciliation after ecommerce sales.
articles.improvement_summary: Expanded the article to 1,135 words before FAQs and CTA. Added official source links, transaction CSV and report roles, payout-ID grouping, parsing rules, lookup tables, duplicate fingerprints, payout and bank checks, exception examples, native-versus-custom guidance, pilot controls and fresh payback maths.
articles.hemingway_readability_check: Passed. The revised article contains 1,135 words before FAQs and CTA. A local plain-language estimate placed the full article body at Flesch-Kincaid grade 8.2, below the grade 9 ceiling.
articles.credibility_repetition_check: Passed. Current Shopify and Xero product claims were checked against official sources. The article names the parsing, lookup, duplicate and tolerance mechanisms and removes vague or repeated guidance.
articles.mobile_layout_check: Passed conceptual checks at about 390px and 768px. Width is fluid, the two-column grid collapses, headings use clamp and CTA text can wrap.
articles.ai_mention_check: Passed. AI is not mentioned because it is not needed for payout reconciliation.
articles.search_console_submission: Not submitted; no Search Console tool was available in this runtime.

articles.title: How to catch product data errors before listings go live
How to stop re-keying wholesale orders into Sage or Xero
Ecommerce AI automation: how to prove the ROI first

The Monday report your ecommerce team should not be building by hand
Before you hire another ecommerce admin, check the process first
How to automate supplier price lists for Shopify, Amazon and wholesale channels

quality_rules: Write for UK ecommerce and wholesale operators first.
Every article must read at Hemingway grade 9 or lower.
Do not invent results, statistics, dates, names or testimonials.
Every article needs a clear workflow, decision point or ROI calculation.
AI is one controlled tool, never the offer or a replacement for judgement.
